divergence: breakfast cereal was never invented — morning food is last night's dinner and the mascots were never born
story_angle: solemn obituary for a beloved institution nobody can remember the purpose of
headline: National Morning Meal Standards Board Officially Defunded, Leaves Behind Mountain of Reheated Meatloaf
deck: Ghost of Bureaucracy Haunts Silver Spring as Legacy of Lunch-for-Breakfast Oversight Comes to a Grim Halt
article:
In an unexpected move resembling the quiet demolition of an abandoned shopping mall, the National Morning Meal Standards Board (NMMBSB) has been defunded, marking the end of its 213-year reign over reheated leftovers as breakfast fare. The final chair, Dr. Eleanor Briggs, delivered her last report in 2024, showcasing the staggering 3.2 million tons of last night's meatloaf dutifully reheated to precisely 165°F in compliance with federal guidelines.

The agency's staff of 47 will now be reassigned to the Department of Reheated Side Dishes, maintaining their GS-13 pay grades, much to the relief of the Dinner-for-Breakfast Caucus. "We have worked tirelessly to ensure that meatloaf meets its full potential as morning sustenance," stated former board member, Timothy P. Leftover, as he packed up his desk filled with 48 different varieties of cold gravy packets.

Originally housed in a windowless annex in Silver Spring, the board's headquarters still proudly displayed a 1978 organizational chart for 'cereal-box liner compliance'—a testament to its historical inertia. Despite administrative decay, it persisted through phantom budgets and meticulous meetings about the viscosity of morning gravy, which now languishes as the sole remnant of a bygone era of bureaucratic excess.

Survived only by the Interagency Reheating Protocol Working Group and a $2.4 million line item for ongoing gravy viscosity studies, the NMMBSB's demise leaves citizens of this meatloaf nation to navigate a lifetime of last-night's dinners without federal reheating charts.
author: Clarence T. Griddle, Bureaucratic Affairs Correspondent

weather.condition: Leftovers Rain with a Side of Gravy

oped.title: Defunding the Board: A Recipe for National Indigestion
oped.author: Horace F. Grumbleton III, PhD in Reheated Entrees & Former Vice-Chair, National Morning Meal Standards Board
oped.body: As the board's longest-serving vice-chair until last week, I find this defunding shortsighted in the extreme. Who else ensured yesterday's pot roast reached a precise 165 degrees by 7 a.m.? Now Silver Spring drowns in unregulated meatloaf, and the rest of us suffer at home with unevenly microwaved leftovers. The real culprits are those citizens wearing improper hats that distract from proper reheating protocols. My own breakfasts have grown cold and flavorless since the closure, forcing me to endure substandard meals like a common civilian. Rather than disbanding oversight, Congress should expand the board's mandate to cover afternoon snacks and midnight snacks too. Anything less invites culinary anarchy. My pension depends on it.

classifieds.text: For Sale: 47 industrial meatloaf reheaters and 12 pallets of 'approved' aluminum trays from the defunded National Morning Meal Standards Board. Cash only, Silver Spring warehouse, ghost sightings not included but likely.

Help Wanted: Night-shift meatloaf redistributor needed for pop-up breakfast co-op. Must handle reheated leftovers and occasional bureaucratic apparitions; no cereal experience required since it never existed.

Lost & Found: Missing binder titled 'Morning Meal Oversight Protocols 2025' last seen before the Board vanished. Reward is two trays of lukewarm meatloaf and a promise the ghost won't follow you home.

Public Notice: Former NMMSB deputy apologizes for the mountain of reheated meatloaf now haunting Silver Spring; free disposal services offered to anyone willing to sign a waiver about spectral leftovers.

panels.caption: Silver Spring door slams shut on a towering pile of stamped reheated meatloaf.
"No more forms," mutters the last clerk. "Just the slabs."
A kid forks a cold slice: "At least the gravy doesn't need a permit."

joke.setup: After the National Morning Meal Standards Board got its funding yanked,
joke.punchline: Americans can finally stop pretending yesterday’s meatloaf counts as a balanced breakfast just because some bureaucrat stamped the reheating chart.
joke.text: After the National Morning Meal Standards Board got its funding yanked, Americans can finally stop pretending yesterday’s meatloaf counts as a balanced breakfast just because some bureaucrat stamped the reheating chart.

sponsor_ads.headline: ReheatNow Pro: Government Surplus Edition
sponsor_ads.body: Direct from the defunded Board's abandoned warehouses, our microwaves come pre-loaded with 47 meatloaf temperature presets and zero nutritional liability. Finally, breakfast that remembers what it was yesterday.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not responsible for existential déjà vu.

sponsor_ads.headline: Morning Loaf Locator
sponsor_ads.body: GPS app that maps the nearest pile of reheated Board meatloaf before it develops sentience in the July heat. Real-time alerts for when the gravy starts moving on its own.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Breakfast is wherever the Board forgot to look.

sponsor_ads.headline: Mascot Replacement Service
sponsor_ads.body: Rent a live raccoon in a tiny apron to stare at your meatloaf until you finish it. Because 2026 mornings still need something to blame when the reheated dinner fights back.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No cartoon characters were ever invented for this.

sponsor_ads.headline: Defunded Breakfast Bonds
sponsor_ads.body: Invest in literal slices of the former National Morning Meal Standards Board's meatloaf reserve. Redeemable for 0% interest and the faint smell of policy failure.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Past performance is literally just last night's dinner.

stories.headline: Leftovers Logistics Firm Reports Record Q3 as Defunded Board Shifts Oversight to Private Haulers
stories.deck: Silver Spring warehouses now stockpile Tuesday's pot roast for Wednesday's 6 a.m. shifts
stories.article:
With the National Morning Meal Standards Board shuttered, ReheatPath Inc. has expanded its fleet of insulated vans to deliver 12 million portions of chilled beef stew and chicken cacciatore to suburban homes each dawn. The company’s CEO cited a 300 percent surge in contracts from office parks where employees once relied on the board’s portion guidelines.

Analysts note that without federal mandates for safe reheating temperatures, firms like ReheatPath now set their own thresholds, leading to a patented “meatloaf equilibrium” process that guarantees no more than 47 degrees Fahrenheit variance during transit from kitchen to cubicle.

real_world_echo: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests — mirrored in the White House quietly dissolving the Board after years of budget protests from the Dinner-for-Breakfast Caucus, with the obituary treating the move as the dignified end of a once-vital command structure
